{"society_power": 8.5, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Brexit referendum and austerity amid civic mobilization surges", "reasoning": "Austerity hollowed out intermediary civil-society institutions (local press, councils, voluntary bodies) and polarization strained cohesion, slightly weakening society despite episodic mass mobilizations (Scotland, Brexit); the state's coercive/surveillance reach expanded (Investigatory Powers Act, counterterror) but this was offset by austerity-driven service cuts, Brexit administrative uncertainty, and devolution, leaving state power roughly stable with judicial checks intact."}
